title: Reply with exactly: explorer OK
summary_text: 1. `doctor` under-reports staleness and recovery progress - Gap: `[scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh)` (`cmd_doctor`) and `[scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh)` only surface coarse `state/reason` and a short stale/active summary. Long-running hangs (retrying, blocked, degraded) are not time-framed. - Patch idea: Add explicit elapsed metrics (`time_in_state`, `since_last_event`, `since_last_success`, retry count/age) in both surface and per-run sections; expose threshold-based warnings (e.g. stale > Xm, retrying > Yn). 2. Failure causes are not operator-actionable enough - Gap: `[scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh)` and `[scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-status-surface.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-status-surface.sh)` compute reasons but don’t preserve recent causal chain (which transition failed first, how long each step took). - Patch idea: Add `doctor` output of last 1–3 transition events per run with timestamps and a compact “failure chain” (e.g., bootstrap→ledger→compact). 3. Status surface is aggregate-heavy, not long-run diagnostic-friendly - Gap: `[scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-status-surface.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-status-surface.sh)` only summarizes counts (running/retrying/degraded/etc.), and operator sees trends only implicitly. - Patch idea: Add a top-N list of the most problematic runs (oldest first), plus per-state duration buckets and highest-risk flags (`stuck_for`, `retry_loop_depth`, `handoff_blocked`). 4. Ledger is written but not surfaced for root-cause diagnosis - Gap: `[scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-ledger.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-ledger.sh)` writes compact event records, but `doctor --run` only prints limited recent events and no SLA-style aging. - Patch idea: Add a `doctor --events --json` or `--run --events --since` view with machine-filtered timeline fields (state durations, event age, actor/reason). 5. Canary run has weak operator UX beyond exit status - Gap: `[scripts/harness/run-canary.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/run-canary.sh)` and CI wrapper `[.github/workflows/fugue-orchestrator-canary.yml](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.github/workflows/fugue-orchestrator-canary.yml)` do not emit a durable, first-class status artifact summarizing step-level failures and recovery attempts. - Patch idea: Write a structured canary report file (JSON + markdown) with run id, case id, elapsed time, failing stage, evidence log links, and emit it as a workflow artifact on both pass/fail. 6. Timeout path does not guide recovery - Gap: timeout handling in `[scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh)` sets failure status but does not show immediate operational next actions. - Patch idea: On timeout, print explicit recovery commands and the top suspects (stale longest run, blocked handoff, highest retry depth) with copy/paste remediation hints. 7. Test coverage validates presence, not observability quality - Gap: `[tests/test-codex-kernel-guard-doctor.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-codex-kernel-guard-doctor.sh)` and `[tests/test-codex-kernel-guard-doctor-flags.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-codex-kernel-guard-doctor-flags.sh)` mostly assert static output chunks, not semantics like durations, state age, or stuck-run ranking; canary test coverage is scenario-level only (`tests/test-kernel-canary-plan.sh`). - Patch idea: Add tests asserting new diagnostic fields (timeout age, per-state duration, stuck-run ranking, JSON schema keys) to lock in operator-visible behavior. Sma...
